talks.title: The H in Computer Science Stands for Human
talks.speaker: Lucas Plabst

questions.question: What does the field of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) study?
options: How to build faster computer processors
How people use, experience, and are frustrated by technology
How to secure networked systems from intrusion
How computers simulate human reasoning

questions.question: Hick's Law in UX design states that the time to make a decision does what as the number of choices increases?
options: Decreases — more options makes choosing easier
Stays constant — humans are efficient decision-makers
Increases logarithmically — more choices means longer deliberation
Increases exponentially — overwhelming users past 5 options

questions.question: What principle explains why users often need 10 clicks to cancel a subscription they signed up for in 2?
options: The Fogg Behavior Model — designers maximize persuasion on sign-up, minimize it on exit
Dark patterns — intentional design choices that mislead users into actions against their interest
Conway's Law — organizations build products that mirror their internal communication structure
Affordance theory — users expect complex tasks to require complex interfaces

talks.title: Unraveling the Art of AI-Generated Crochet Patterns
talks.speaker: Charlotte Connolly

questions.question: Standard crochet patterns are written in what format?
options: A grid-based color chart
Abbreviated written instructions using stitch codes (e.g., "ch 3, sc in next st")
Musical notation repurposed for stitch timing
Mathematical equations for generating curves

questions.question: When a large language model generates a crochet pattern, it does so by doing what?
options: Simulating the physics of yarn tension and stitch geometry
Predicting the next most probable token based on patterns in training data
Retrieving the closest matching pattern from a structured database
Comparing aesthetic goals against a library of 3D crochet models

questions.question: Crochet is uniquely capable of modeling hyperbolic surfaces (like coral reefs or lettuce edges) because of what structural property?
options: Each new row can be made longer than the previous row, creating exponential outward curvature
Crochet stitches are topologically equivalent to tori, enabling negative Gaussian curvature
The slip stitch can encode negative curvature at the individual stitch level
Double crochet creates a lattice that is isomorphic to hyperbolic tessellations

originStory.text:
Hi. I'm Justin Fritz — and I'm a Nerd Nite Boss.

That's a real title. The Nerd Nite organization gave it to me, and I take it very seriously.

Nerd Nite started in Boston in 2003. Graduate student Chris Balakrishnan had been telling the same stories at his local bar — The Midway Cafe — about studying parasitic birds in Cameroon. The bartenders finally told him: just give a talk. One night. Slides. Get it out of your system.

He did. It worked.

In 2006, his roommate Matt Wasowski moved the concept to New York City, expanded it beyond science to any topic worth a drink and 20 minutes, and built it into a global brand — the "Discovery Channel with beer" — now in over 100 cities worldwide.

It came to Fort Collins because of me and one other very important person: Senne Van Loon.

But first — trivia.

Senne and I built this thing from scratch.

Senne is the kind of person who hears "let's put on a nerdy event at a bar" and says yes immediately — full commitment, no hesitation. That energy is rare and it matters. You cannot build something like this alone.

As we grew, Jamie Fritz joined us. Jamie is the backbone — always behind the scenes, making everything work, never needing the spotlight. The kind of person every organization needs and rarely thanks enough.

Jamie has been onstage exactly once: to introduce Marybeth Arcodia. Which brings me to Marybeth.

Marybeth Arcodia was our host.

She was brilliant at it — warm, sharp, funny in exactly the right way for a room full of nerds on their third drink. She held the stage like she was born for it.

Marybeth eventually left Fort Collins for a faculty position in Miami. We were sad to lose her and enormously proud of her.

Hannah Bauer stepped up as host after Marybeth — and she's been incredible.

Here's the thing you need to understand about Nerd Nite: once you're a Boss, you're always a Boss. Marybeth is still a Boss. Distance doesn't change that.

You just played Nerdometer.

Every edition is built around a real Nerd Nite Fort Collins event — the actual talks, the actual speakers, the real nerdy obsessions people bring to a bar and share with strangers.

Senne. Jamie. Marybeth. Hannah. Every speaker who ever trusted a room full of people with the thing they love most.

All Bosses. Always.

Come be part of it.

— Justin Fritz, Nerd Nite Fort Collins Boss
